summary: The prenatal skin atlas identifies Innate T type3 as a fetal-scope cell type in the T cell lineage. T cells emerged later in gestation accompanying thymus formation from around 10 PCW. Pre-dermal condensate cells co-located with dendritic and lymphoid cells.
key_quotes: Innate immune cells, such as macrophages and innate lymphoid cells (ILCs), were present from early gestation, whereas B cells and T cells emerged later, accompanying thymus, bone marrow and spleen formation from around 10 PCW
pre-dermal condensate (pre-Dc) cells co-located with dendritic and lymphoid cells based on correlation analyses

summary: Suo et al. define type 3 innate T cells as PLZF-expressing unconventional T cells with type 3 effector potential. Type 1 and 3 innate T cells were developmental-specific. Treg and type 3 innate T cells were located within both thymic medullary microenvironments. A large proportion of gamma-delta T cells, particularly CD8AA and type 3 innate T cell subtypes, expressed TRGV8 or TRGV10. Unconventional T cells showed more proximal TCR gene usage than conventional T cells.
key_quotes: Type 1 and 3 innate T cells were almost negligible in postnatal thymus, whereas CD8AA T cell abundance rebounded in pediatric age groups before a further decline in adulthood. Thus, type 1 and 3 innate T cells, but not CD8AA, appear to be developmentalspecific unconventional T cells.
Treg and type 3 innate T cells were located within both microenvironments.
there was also a large proportion of gammadelta T cells, particularly those of CD8AA and type 3 innate T cell subtypes, expressing TRGV8 or TRGV10 instead

summary: Dhariwala et al. note limitations in characterizing innate-type T cells in fetal skin but demonstrate that developing human skin contains lymphocytes with a memory signature.
key_quotes: Our ability to comment on innate-type T cells (i.e., MAIT, gd, and NK T cells) in fetal skin was also limited by existing hurdles in combining tetramers with CyTOF and by the total number of fetal skin leukocytes we had to analyze.

summary: Review of fetal T cell tolerance. Innate-like T cells with Th1 and Th17 characteristics exist in the fetus. Fetal semi-invariant gamma-delta T cells are functionally pre-programmed.
key_quotes: A number of innate-like T cells capable of Th1 cytokine production are present in the human fetus, including gammadelta T cells, invariant natural killer T (iNKT) cells, and mucosa-associated invariant T (MAIT) cells.

summary: Spidale et al. demonstrate that neonatal-derived IL-17-producing dermal gamma-delta T cells prevent spontaneous atopic dermatitis, highlighting the role of type 3 innate-like T cells in skin homeostasis.

Sanchez Sanchez et al. identify distinct functional thymic programming of fetal gamma-delta thymocytes. Mature fetal gamma-delta thymocytes are committed to type 1, type 3, or type 2-like effector fates in a wave-like pattern depending on gestation age.
key_quotes: Mature fetal gammadelta thymocytes (both the Vgamma9Vdelta2 and nonVgamma9Vdelta2 subsets) are committed to either a type 1, a type 3 or a type 2-like effector fate displaying a wave-like pattern depending on gestation age
